data.title: Microservices Structure
data.description: Spotify employs a microservices architecture to facilitate independent service deployment and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Each function (e.g., playlist management, music streaming) is compartmentalized into services that can be individually developed, scaled, and maintained.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN) Integration
data.description: CDNs are used to distribute high-quality music and video content efficiently to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes global CDN providers for low latency and high availability of media streaming.

data.title: Data-Driven Recommendation System
data.description: Spotify's recommendation engine utilizes big data analytics to curate personalized playlists for users.
data.technical_spec: Incorporates machine learning models trained on user listening data to suggest new or popular tracks.

data.title: Distributed Database Management
data.description: Employs distributed database systems to handle large volumes of data with high availability.
data.technical_spec: Managed using technologies like Cassandra for scalability across multiple servers and regions.

data.title: API Gateway
data.description: Acts as a single entry point for API calls, routing them to appropriate microservices.
data.technical_spec: Facilitates communication across services while providing load balancing and request filtering.
